_notes: Flat GRPO baseline for AlfWorld SOTA — quantifies the credit-assignment lift contributed by TurnRDV2's per-turn signal vs the pure trajectory-level group baseline. Recipe-matched to configs/TurnRDV2_alfworld_SOTA_10round_mlpr32_v3.json on every other axis (env, dense intermediate_reward signals, K=8, lr=5e-6, kl=0.04, rank-32 LoRA + MLP target modules, num_train_games=400, 10 rounds × 80 eps via launcher, eval pool [6500, 6600), warm-start adapter family). Only experimental variables that differ: (a) hgpo.alpha=1.0 (drops the per-turn signal entirely — A_H = A_traj), (b) hgpo.decomposer='progress' (no TurnRD model is built, no V-head, no replay buffer, no train_turnrd call per round — saves ~5 min/round and removes a learnable component that's mathematically inert at α=1.0), (c) hgpo.use_v_projection_for_decomposition / v_projection_clamp / turnrd block all DROPPED (unused at α=1.0 + decomposer='progress'), (d) seed=41 picks a task-id slice [32800, 33600) for rounds=10/eps=80 that is disjoint from v1 (seed=11 -> [5280, 5760)), v2 (seed=23 -> [14720, 15360)), v3 (seed=31 -> [24800, 25600)), goalsup variants, and eval ([6500, 6600)). Disjoint task slice + disjoint run-name prefix + no Modal volume cache collisions (no /vol/cache/turnrd_* paths needed). Launcher: scripts/run_flatGRPO_alfworld_SOTA_a100.sh — per-round bash for-loop over `modal run infra/app_train_loop.py::train_loop_alfworld` (NOT the TurnRD orchestrator, which requires decomposer='turnrd'). NOTE: this is NOT 'Progress HGPO' (Method C). `progress_decomposer` (src/algorithms/grpo/trainer.py:131) IS the Method-C callable that emits per-turn raw_env_reward, but Method C runs it at alpha<1.0 so (1-alpha)*A_turn carries the per-turn signal. Here alpha=1.0 multiplies A_turn by zero in `combine` (src/algorithms/grpo/advantage.py:130), and lambda_consistency=0.0 keeps it out of the consistency loss — the per-turn rewards are computed and discarded, exactly equivalent to pure flat GRPO. We pick 'progress' (vs 'turnrd') purely because it's parameter-free and skips the TurnRD compute. A real Progress-HGPO baseline at alpha=0.5 + decomposer='progress' would be a separate config. Expected outcome based on the existing alpha-sweep (reports/alfworld_alpha_sweep_README.md): α=0.50 mean=0.540, α=0.75 mean=0.518, α=0.25 mean=0.514. The α=1.0 mean should land near α=0.75 (extrapolating the unimodal pattern). The credit-assignment lift is then estimated as (α=0.50 best mean) − (α=1.0 best mean). Plan: turn_adv_noise_followup.
